meta.title: magni

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: magni
root.mentor_note: magni is a grand root, and most of the words built on it were made to sound grand too.

attempt.direction_prompt: A modest, ordinary building, or an impressive, splendid one?

context_sentences: The cathedral’s ceiling was magnificent, painted from end to end without a single repeated figure.
Guests still talk about the magnificent view from the terrace, even years after the wedding.
The lens could magnify a single grain of pollen until it filled the entire screen.
One careless remark, magnified by the rumour mill, had become a full blown scandal by Friday.
In victory he was magnanimous, praising the opponent he had beaten by a single point.
The retiring CEO made a magnanimous gesture, handing her own bonus to the junior staff.

construct_options.text: Generous and forgiving, especially toward a rival or the less powerful
Physically enormous in size or scale
Petty and unwilling to forgive a rival

context_sentences: Nobody grasped the magnitude of the damage until the first aerial photos came in.
An earthquake of that magnitude had not struck the region in over a century.

construct_options.text: The great size, extent, or importance of something
A lens used to make small objects appear larger
A generous and forgiving attitude toward a rival
